title: Node 24, TypeScript 7, and amqplib 2.0.1 Contract · Contract Lens: Clean Client Build
summary: A service mixes callback and promise APIs, assumes publish returns a Promise, trusts TypeScript types as runtime validation, and exits before asynchronous close and confirms settle. Planned artifact: Add and verify the clean client build increment in typed amqplib lifecycle and boundary contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prove clean install, strict type checking, deterministic tests, compiled artifact, connection configuration, and graceful teardown under changed inputs.
Own connection, channel, return, drain, close, error, blocked, unblocked, cancellation, and handler-error events without unhandled process failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Clean Client Build · Theory Clean Client Build
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 9382 in a consumer fleet under a shared dependency outage. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate a typed amqplib client without treating types or publish booleans as broker proof; keep every queue and subscriber responsibility explicitly named; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove clean install, strict type checking, deterministic tests, compiled artifact, connection configuration, and graceful teardown under changed inputs.
